# WI-QUIC-CRT-0034 - QUIC CRT client-side abbreviated resumption completion work item

## Summary

Advance accepted PSK ClientHello attempts through abbreviated client-side handshake completion while keeping rejected attempts on the existing full-handshake fallback and early data closed.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0137

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0033

## Planned Changes

- Advance accepted PSK ClientHello attempts through the abbreviated client-side `EncryptedExtensions -> Finished` handshake flight.
- Keep rejected attempts on the existing full-handshake fallback path rather than forking a second recovery path.
- Preserve the existing early-data-closed behavior and avoid widening the public resumption contract.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for accepted abbreviated completion, rejected fallback, transcript shape, and early-data closure.

## Out of Scope

- 0-RTT packet send/receive support
- anti-replay behavior
- key update
- public API widening
- IsSupported widening
- broad post-handshake TLS support
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to accepted-branch completion

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for accepted abbreviated completion, rejected fallback, abbreviated transcript omission of certificate and certificate-verify, early-data closure, and the public no-resumption/no-early-data guard; then run the relevant CRT/API/INT guard set, the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep, and SpecTrace render/validation checks.

## Completion Notes

The managed client/runtime path now advances accepted PSK ClientHello attempts through the abbreviated `EncryptedExtensions -> Finished` resumption flight, keeps rejected attempts on the existing full-handshake fallback path, and keeps early data explicitly closed without widening the public surface.
